Valuation is unambiguously cheap: trailing P/E of 7.98× sits well below the Energy sector median of 8–14× (at the very low end), EV/EBITDA of 4.56× is deeply discounted for a capital-intensive upstream business, and P/B of 0.89× with ROE of 12.7% signals the stock trades below book despite generating decent returns — the forward P/E of 6.05× below trailing implies the market expects earnings recovery in FY27. The biggest risk is that rising crude at $93.65/bbl is a double-edged sword for ONGC — it boosts realisations but also re-introduces upstream profit-sharing and subsidy burden risk from downstream PSUs, and the high debt/equity of 42.55× leaves limited buffer if government levies or cess rates increase.

Trailing P/E of 55.6× sits right at the Consumer Discretionary sector median (55.6×, P50 peer rank), which is at the upper end of the FMCG/Consumer benchmark range of 40–60×, while EV/EBITDA of 36.9× is actually below the sector peer median of 44.2× (P29), and the forward P/E of 44.4× below trailing signals modest earnings growth is priced in — overall a fair valuation with limited margin of safety at current levels. The single biggest risk is that mid-single-digit revenue growth (~6%) with margin pressure and a 34% OCF decline suggests demand has plateaued in the premium innerwear segment, and any further cost inflation (labour codes, cotton prices) or consumption slowdown could compress earnings meaningfully at a 55× P/E multiple.
